epigraph.quote: Corners of towers cut away, so platforms for cows to look out.

per_stop.quote: As soon as we arrived I put the kettle on (5.10.) + then did yoga for 10 min + felt much less stiff.
Laon Cathedral is beautiful, especially the Tower of the crossing from inside!
We drove thro the Laonnaise hills, skirting Champagne, then crossed a series of rivers such as Aisne, Marne + Seine on Droats.
At one time 1000 pilgrims were burned when the cathedral caught fire.
Simon didn't come into one of the most interesting churches with this theme because it had a "no shorts" sign.
There were 2 layers of mist on the reservoir.
We have now reached a v. entertaining one, bursting with noisy children.
Men are dismantling a village marquee after yesterday's festival.
Every pedal + push, steady incline. Only 1½ hours to get back, free-wheeling nearly all the way.
Late, about 8.30, we walked up into town & saw trestle tables & lots of people eating in the square & local produce on sale.
We chose a restaurant on the square which advertised puddings made in the restaurant. It was a very good choice.
We drove to Figeac, looked at the Rosetta Stone, had good coffee in the covered market & shopped.
There is a pool, a lovely walnut orchard
We had crepes & beer & swam in the lake.
When Simon opened it there was only her old passport inside it.
